title: Centralized configuration module with typed defaults, env var overrides, and config file support
close_reason: PR #15 created, verified, reviewed. 222 tests pass (187 existing + 35 new). Config module with typed dataclass, env var overrides, TOML support, and 4-layer merge precedence.
agent_context: {"objective":"Create a centralized config.py module that consolidates all hardcoded defaults (MAX_SESSIONS, db_path, retention period, API host/port, prometheus settings) into a typed dataclass with env var overrides and optional TOML config file support","constraints":{"MUST":["Use dataclass with typed fields for all settings","Support environment variable overrides via TPS_COUNTER_ prefix (e.g. TPS_COUNTER_MAX_SESSIONS=100)","Support optional TOML config file at ~/.hermes/plugins/tps-counter/config.toml","Provide get_config() function that merges defaults < config file < env vars","Replace all hardcoded values in __init__.py and store.py with config references","Maintain backward compatibility: existing ctx.get_config() still works as override source","All defaults must match current hardcoded values exactly","Thread-safe config loading (lazy init with lock)"],"SHOULD":["Use Python 3.11+ dataclass features","Provide config validation with sensible error messages","Auto-create config directory if missing","Document all config keys in docstrings"]},"files":{"allowed":["config.py","__init__.py","store.py","tests/"],"forbidden":[".pi/","README.md","HERMES.md","api.py"]}}

title: Add core plugin behavior tests for TPS calculation, session management, and lifecycle
close_reason: PR #12 created. 43 new tests covering all 15 PRD requirements. Full suite: 149 pass, 0 fail.
agent_context: {"objective":"Add comprehensive pytest tests for core plugin behavior: TPS calculation (_SessionTPS), per-model tracking (_ModelTPS, get_model_stats), per-provider stats (get_provider_stats), session lifecycle (_cleanup_session, _evict_if_needed, _on_session_end), utility functions (_extract_provider, _fmt_tokens, summary_line), get_tps_stats, and status bar snapshot building","constraints":{"MUST":["Test _SessionTPS.record() with various input/output token combos","Test avg_tps, peak_tps, turn_tps calculations","Test _ModelTPS tracking and get_model_stats()","Test get_provider_stats() wiring","Test _cleanup_session removes all state (sessions + models + providers + DB)","Test _evict_if_needed respects MAX_SESSIONS limit","Test _on_session_end triggers cleanup","Test _extract_provider with LiteLLM-style model strings","Test _fmt_tokens for K/M formatting","Test summary_line output format","Test get_tps_stats returns correct dict structure","Test status bar snapshot construction in _on_post_api_request","All tests use temp DB, clean up global state in fixtures","Thread safety: concurrent hook calls dont corrupt state"],"SHOULD":["Test edge cases: zero tokens, zero duration, missing session_id","Test _hydrate_from_db loads from persistent store","Test _persist_state writes through to SQLite"]},"files":{"allowed":["tests/","__init__.py","conftest.py"],"forbidden":[".pi/","README.md","HERMES.md","store.py","api.py"]}}

title: Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to REST API endpoints to protect SQLite backend from burst requests
description:
Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to REST API endpoints to protect the SQLite backend from burst requests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Burst requests from one IP to protected REST endpoints receive HTTP 429 with Retry-After before handler/store work.
- Under-limit requests preserve existing REST response contracts.
- TPSConfig supports requests_per_minute and burst_size via defaults, TOML, env, and context overrides with validation.
- /api/v1/health remains exempt from throttling.
- Prometheus exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total after throttled requests when prometheus_client is available.
- Affected API/config/rate-limiting/Prometheus tests pass without new dependencies.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Burst requests from one IP to protected REST endpoints receive HTTP 429 with Retry-After before handler/store work.
- Under-limit requests preserve existing REST response contracts.
- TPSConfig supports requests_per_minute and burst_size via defaults, TOML, env, and context overrides with validation.
- /api/v1/health remains exempt from throttling.
- Prometheus exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total after throttled requests when prometheus_client is available.
- Affected API/config/rate-limiting/Prometheus tests pass without new dependencies.
notes: COMPLETED: Added per-IP rate limiting middleware, TPSConfig rate limit settings, Prometheus throttling counter, and focused tests. Verified with affected pytest suites.
agent_context: {"objective":"Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to all REST API endpoints so burst requests cannot overwhelm the SQLite backend or degrade monitoring reliability.","constraints":{"MUST":["Do not change existing endpoint response contracts for non-rate-limited requests","Return proper HTTP 429 responses with Retry-After header when limits exceeded","Make rate limits configurable via TPSConfig (requests_per_minute, burst_size)","Expose a Prometheus counter for rate-limited requests (tps_api_rate_limited_total)","Do not add external dependencies (no Redis, no slowapi — use stdlib/collections-based sliding window"],"SHOULD":["Use a simple sliding window counter per IP address","Clean up stale IP entries to prevent memory leaks","Log rate limit events at DEBUG level","Support exempting health endpoint from rate limiting"]},"files":{"allowed":["api.py","config.py","prometheus_metrics.py","tests/test_api.py","tests/test_config.py","tests/test_rate_limiting.py"],"forbidden":[".beads/beads.db",".env.local","credentials","plan.md","tasks.md","context-capsule.md"]}}

comments.author: daedalus
comments.text: Verification: python -m pytest tests/test_api.py tests/test_config.py tests/test_rate_limiting.py tests/test_prometheus.py -x -q => 119 passed

title: Add built-in TPS dashboard HTML page served at root path for real-time monitoring without external tools
agent_context: {"objective":"Serve a lightweight built-in HTML dashboard at the API root path (/) so users can monitor TPS in real-time via WebSocket without external tools.","constraints":{"MUST":["Serve HTML at GET / when API is enabled","Use vanilla JS only — zero external dependencies","Consume existing /ws/tps WebSocket and /api/v1/* REST endpoints","Dashboard must work offline once loaded (no CDN)","Do not break existing /docs or /api/v1/* routes","HTML embedded as Python string or single file in package"],"SHOULD":["Auto-reconnect WebSocket on disconnect","Show session list, TPS sparkline, model breakdown","Responsive layout for mobile/desktop","Graceful degradation if WebSocket unavailable — fall back to polling REST"]},"files":{"allowed":["api.py","dashboard.py","tests/test_dashboard.py","README.md"],"forbidden":[".beads/beads.db",".env.local","credentials","plan.md","tasks.md","context-capsule.md"]}}

title: Add comprehensive health diagnostics endpoint for plugin runtime state visibility
close_reason: PR #20 created, verified (289 tests pass, 0 regressions), reviewed
agent_context: {"objective":"Add a comprehensive GET /api/v1/health/diagnostics endpoint that reports runtime state of all plugin components: in-memory sessions, SQLite persistence, Prometheus metrics, WebSocket connections, and operational health counters. Replace the existing basic health check with a richer diagnostic view.","constraints":{"MUST":["New endpoint GET /api/v1/health/diagnostics returns JSON with component status breakdown","Reports: memory_sessions (count, max), sqlite (connected, session_count, event_count), prometheus (enabled, metrics_count), websocket (enabled, active_connections), health_counters (all operational counters)","Each component has status (ok/degraded/unavailable) and detail fields","Backward compatible: existing GET /api/v1/health endpoint unchanged","Thread-safe reads from all state sources","Graceful degradation: components that fail report degraded status, not crash","Tests for all component states and the combined response"],"SHOULD":["Add optional ?verbose=true query param for extended detail (per-model breakdown, DB file size)","Response includes uptime_seconds for the API server","Include plugin version and config summary (non-sensitive fields only)"]},"files":{"allowed":["api.py","tests/test_api.py","README.md"],"forbidden":[".pi/","HERMES.md","store.py","config.py","prometheus_metrics.py","__init__.py"]}}

title: Add operational health metrics for plugin self-monitoring — error counters, health gauges, and diagnostic signals in Prometheus
close_reason: PR created, verified, reviewed. All 234 tests pass with zero regressions.
agent_context: {"objective":"Add Prometheus counters and gauges that track the plugin operational health: usage extraction failures, DB read/write errors, WebSocket broadcast failures, dead client removals, and active connection count. These metrics let operators monitor plugin reliability alongside TPS data.","constraints":{"MUST":["Add counters in prometheus_metrics.py for: usage_extraction_failures_total, db_write_errors_total, db_read_errors_total, ws_broadcast_failures_total, ws_dead_clients_total","Add gauge: ws_active_connections (current WebSocket client count)","Increment counters from __init__.py (_on_post_api_request, _persist_state, _hydrate_from_db) and api.py (ConnectionManager)","All new metrics use the existing custom REGISTRY","Thread-safe: counter increments must be safe for concurrent hook calls","Tests for all new metrics","Backward compatible with existing tests and Prometheus output"],"SHOULD":["Add a /api/v1/health detail endpoint that includes error rates","Include HELP and TYPE metadata for each new metric","Use labels for error categorization where useful","Document new metrics in README"]},"files":{"allowed":["prometheus_metrics.py","__init__.py","api.py","tests/","README.md"],"forbidden":[".pi/","HERMES.md","store.py","config.py"]}}

title: Add Prometheus cardinality guardrails to avoid unbounded session_id time series
close_reason: PR #19 created, 242/242 tests pass, verified bounded cardinality
agent_context: {"objective":"Reduce Prometheus time-series cardinality risk by adding guardrails around unbounded session_id/model/provider labels while preserving useful TPS observability.","constraints":{"MUST":["Do not add new unbounded labels to Prometheus metrics","Provide a bounded-label or aggregate metric strategy for session/model/provider dimensions","Preserve existing /metrics availability and graceful degradation when prometheus_client is absent","Document cardinality behavior and compatibility implications","Add regression coverage for bounded cardinality behavior and metrics output"],"SHOULD":["Prefer aggregate gauges/counters/histograms without session_id where per-session detail is not required","If legacy session-labeled metrics remain, make them explicitly configurable and disabled or capped by default","Expose enough aggregate metrics for Grafana dashboards without requiring per-session labels"]},"files":{"allowed":["prometheus_metrics.py","__init__.py","config.py","tests/test_prometheus.py","README.md"],"forbidden":[".pi/",".beads/beads.db","HERMES.md"]}}

title: Add Prometheus Histogram metrics for TPS and API latency distribution tracking (p50/p95/p99)
notes: COMPLETED: Added TPS and API latency Prometheus histograms with model labels, helper observe functions, hook integration, tests, and README docs. Verified with pytest tests/ -x (294 passed).
agent_context: {"objective":"Add Prometheus Histogram metrics for TPS (tok/s) and API call latency (seconds) to enable p50/p95/p99 distribution tracking. Use the existing custom REGISTRY and follow the graceful-degradation pattern (optional prometheus_client). Add per-model labels where practical.","constraints":{"MUST":["Add Histogram: tps_distribution (buckets: [1, 5, 10, 25, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000]) with labels: model","Add Histogram: api_call_latency_seconds (buckets: [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10, 30, 60]) with labels: model","Both histograms use the existing custom REGISTRY in prometheus_metrics.py","Record observations from _on_post_api_request in __init__.py","Graceful degradation: all histogram code guarded by _PROMETHEUS_AVAILABLE","Tests for histogram registration, observation recording, and /metrics output","Backward compatible with existing tests and Prometheus output"],"SHOULD":["Add helper function observe_tps(value, model) and observe_latency(seconds, model) in prometheus_metrics.py","Include HELP and TYPE metadata for each histogram","Document new metrics in README Prometheus section"]},"files":{"allowed":["prometheus_metrics.py","__init__.py","tests/test_prometheus.py","README.md"],"forbidden":[".pi/","HERMES.md","store.py","config.py","api.py"]}}

title: WebSocket real-time streaming endpoint for live TPS data updates
close_reason: PR #14 created. 187/187 tests pass (174 existing + 13 new WebSocket tests). ConnectionManager, /ws/tps endpoint, hook broadcast integration, all acceptance criteria met.
agent_context: {"objective":"Add a WebSocket endpoint (/ws/tps) that streams real-time TPS updates to connected clients after each LLM API call. Implement a ConnectionManager to track connected clients, broadcast TPS snapshots on hook calls, and handle connection lifecycle gracefully.","constraints":{"MUST":["Add /ws/tps WebSocket endpoint to the existing FastAPI app in api.py","Implement ConnectionManager class to track connect/disconnect/broadcast","Broadcast TPS snapshot (last_tps, avg_tps, peak_tps, tokens, model, provider) to all clients on each hook call","Handle WebSocket disconnect gracefully (remove from manager, no crashes)","Thread-safe: ConnectionManager must be safe for concurrent hook calls from _on_post_api_request","Add JSON message format with type field for future extensibility","Tests for ConnectionManager (connect, disconnect, broadcast, error handling)","Tests for WebSocket endpoint (connect, receive message, disconnect)","Backward compatible with existing tests and REST API"],"SHOULD":["Support optional channel filtering (subscribe to specific session/model/provider)","Include heartbeat/ping to detect stale connections","Rate-limit broadcasts to avoid flooding slow clients","Log connection count at debug level"]},"files":{"allowed":["api.py","__init__.py","tests/","conftest.py"],"forbidden":[".pi/","README.md","HERMES.md","store.py"]}}
